prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins on a nearly black frame.

It then cuts to a view through horizontal dark slats or rails, looking into a dim work area under or near a sink. A middle-aged/older man in an orange shirt is visible behind the slats, surrounded by tools and household items. He appears to be working on plumbing.

The next shots are extreme close-ups of plumbing hardware: a metal nut or pipe fitting, followed by the open end of a pipe or drain part with dark residue visible inside. These close-ups emphasize grime or buildup in the plumbing.

The man is then shown beside a red toolbox, gesturing or handling something near it. He is later seen standing in a kitchen or utility area, wearing the same orange shirt, with cabinets and a doorway in the background.

A white-and-blue bottle labeled “Liquid-Plumr” is placed on a counter next to the red toolbox. The label is partly visible and includes the product name in large red letters.

The setting shifts clearly to a kitchen sink area. The man stands at the counter near the sink, with shelves, bottles, fruit, and other household objects in the background. The Liquid-Plumr bottle is positioned upright on the counter near the faucet.

A close-up shows the man pouring a white liquid from the Liquid-Plumr bottle into a small cap or measuring cup. The product label is visible on the bottle during the pour.

A later frame shows text reading “Three Weeks Later.” over another view through dark horizontal slats, again suggesting an under-sink or plumbing perspective.

The commercial then shows the product bottle in the background while a yellow diagonal graphic strip crosses the frame with bold black text: “BUILD-UP REMOVER.”

The final product shot presents the Liquid-Plumr bottle prominently on the right. Large white text on the left reads: “GETS THE GUNK THAT SLOWS YOUR DRAINS.” The bottle label reads “LIQUID-PLUMR” and “BUILD-UP REMOVER.” The commercial then cuts to black.
